text: What do you want of me?  I'll give you anything you want, but do what I ask!
en: What do you want of me?  I'll give you anything you want, but do what I ask!

text: And what is that Bonasera?
en: And what is that Bonasera?

text: I ask for Justice.
en: I ask for Justice.

text: The Court gave you justice.
en: The Court gave you justice.

text: An eye for an eye!
en: An eye for an eye!

text: But your daughter is still alive.
en: But your daughter is still alive.

text: Then make them suffer as she suffers.  How much shall I pay you.
en: Then make them suffer as she suffers.  How much shall I pay you.

text: America has been good to me...
en: America has been good to me...

text: Then take the justice from the judge, the bitter with the sweet, Bonasera.  But if you come to me with your friendship, your loyalty, then your enemies become my enemies, and then, believe me, they would fear you...
en: Then take the justice from the judge, the bitter with the sweet, Bonasera.  But if you come to me with your friendship, your loyalty, then your enemies become my enemies, and then, believe me, they would fear you...

text: Good.  From me you'll get Justice.
en: Good.  From me you'll get Justice.

text: Godfather.
en: Godfather.

text: Some day, and that day may never come, I would like to call upon you to do me a service in return.
en: Some day, and that day may never come, I would like to call upon you to do me a service in return.

text: What are you, a dance judge?  Go do your job; take a walk around the neighborhood... see everything is okay.
en: What are you, a dance judge?  Go do your job; take a walk around the neighborhood... see everything is okay.

text: Nazorine, my friend, tell me what I can do.
en: Nazorine, my friend, tell me what I can do.

text: Now that the war is over, Enzo, this boy is being repatriated to Italy.  And you see, Godfather...  He...my daughter...they...
en: Now that the war is over, Enzo, this boy is being repatriated to Italy.  And you see, Godfather...  He...my daughter...they...

text: You want him to stay in this country.
en: You want him to stay in this country.

text: Godfather, you understand everything.
en: Godfather, you understand everything.

text: Tom, what we need is an Act of Congress to allow Enzo to become a citizen.
en: Tom, what we need is an Act of Congress to allow Enzo to become a citizen.

text: An Act of Congress!
en: An Act of Congress!

text: You understand him better than anyone.
en: You understand him better than anyone.

text: I told you I had a lot of relatives.
en: I told you I had a lot of relatives.

text: They're waiting to see my father.
en: They're waiting to see my father.

text: They're talking to themselves.
en: They're talking to themselves.

text: They're going to talk to my father, which means they're going to ask him for something, which means they better get it right.
en: They're going to talk to my father, which means they're going to ask him for something, which means they better get it right.

text: Why do they bother him on a day like this?
en: Why do they bother him on a day like this?

text: Because they know that no Sicilian will refuse a request on his daughter's wedding day.
en: Because they know that no Sicilian will refuse a request on his daughter's wedding day.

text: Who is he?
en: Who is he?

text: You really want to know?
en: You really want to know?

text: Yes.  Tell me.
en: Yes.  Tell me.

text: You like spaghetti?
en: You like spaghetti?

text: You know I love spaghetti.
en: You know I love spaghetti.

text: Then eat your spaghetti and I'll tell you a Luca Brasi story.
en: Then eat your spaghetti and I'll tell you a Luca Brasi story.

text: Al Capone!
en: Al Capone!

text: My Father sent Luca Brasi after them.  He tied the two Capone men hand and foot, and stuffed small bath towels into their mouths. Then he took an ax, and chopped one man's feet off...
en: My Father sent Luca Brasi after them.  He tied the two Capone men hand and foot, and stuffed small bath towels into their mouths. Then he took an ax, and chopped one man's feet off...

text: Michael...
en: Michael...

text: Then the legs at the knees...
en: Then the legs at the knees...

text: Michael you're trying to scare me...
en: Michael you're trying to scare me...

text: Then the thighs where they joined the torso.
en: Then the thighs where they joined the torso.

text: Michael, I don't want to hear anymore...
en: Michael, I don't want to hear anymore...

text: Then Luca turned to the other man...
en: Then Luca turned to the other man...

text: Michael, I love you.
en: Michael, I love you.

text: ...who out of sheer terror had swallowed the bath towel in his mouth and suffocated.
en: ...who out of sheer terror had swallowed the bath towel in his mouth and suffocated.

text: I told you you wouldn't like him.
en: I told you you wouldn't like him.

text: He's coming over here!
en: He's coming over here!

text: How do you do.
en: How do you do.

text: My brother, Tom Hagen.
en: My brother, Tom Hagen.

text: Thanks Tom.
en: Thanks Tom.

text: My brother Sonny found him living in the streets when he was a kid, so my father took him in.  He's a good lawyer.
en: My brother Sonny found him living in the streets when he was a kid, so my father took him in.  He's a good lawyer.

text: The Senator called--apologized for not coming personally, but said you'd understand.  Also, some of the Judges...they've all sent gifts. And another call from Virgil Sollozzo.
en: The Senator called--apologized for not coming personally, but said you'd understand.  Also, some of the Judges...they've all sent gifts. And another call from Virgil Sollozzo.

text: And with prison record.
en: And with prison record.

text: Two terms; one in Italy, one in the United States.  He's known to the Government as a top narcotics man. That could be a plus for us; he could never get immunity to testify.
en: Two terms; one in Italy, one in the United States.  He's known to the Government as a top narcotics man. That could be a plus for us; he could never get immunity to testify.

text: When did he call?
en: When did he call?

text: This morning.
en: This morning.

text: On a day like this.  Consiglero, do you also have in your notes the the Turk made his living from Prostitution before the war, like the Tattaglias do now.  Write that down before you forget it.  The Turk will wait.
en: On a day like this.  Consiglero, do you also have in your notes the the Turk made his living from Prostitution before the war, like the Tattaglias do now.  Write that down before you forget it.  The Turk will wait.

text: Should I bring him in.
en: Should I bring him in.

text: No.  Let the people enjoy him.  You see?  He is a good godson.
en: No.  Let the people enjoy him.  You see?  He is a good godson.

text: It's been two years.  He's probably in trouble again.
en: It's been two years.  He's probably in trouble again.

text: Sure.
en: Sure.

text: I used to come down to New York whenever he sang at the Capitol and scream my head off.
en: I used to come down to New York whenever he sang at the Capitol and scream my head off.

text: He's my father's godson; he owes him his whole career.
en: He's my father's godson; he owes him his whole career.

text: Can I do something for you still? You're not too rich, or too famous that I can't help you?
en: Can I do something for you still? You're not too rich, or too famous that I can't help you?

text: I'm not rich anymore, Godfather, and...my career, I'm almost washed up...
en: I'm not rich anymore, Godfather, and...my career, I'm almost washed up...

text: Okay.  One minute.
en: Okay.  One minute.

text: He owns the studio.  Just a month ago he bought the movie rights to this book, a best seller.  And the main character is a guy just like me.  I wouldn't even have to act, just be myself.
en: He owns the studio.  Just a month ago he bought the movie rights to this book, a best seller.  And the main character is a guy just like me.  I wouldn't even have to act, just be myself.

text: Sure.
en: Sure.

text: It's too late.  All the contracts have been signed, they're almost ready to shoot.
en: It's too late.  All the contracts have been signed, they're almost ready to shoot.

text: I'll make him an offer he can't refuse.
en: I'll make him an offer he can't refuse.

text: They'll cut the cake in a few minutes...leave right after that. Your new son-in-law, do we give him something important?
en: They'll cut the cake in a few minutes...leave right after that. Your new son-in-law, do we give him something important?

text: No, give him a living.  But never let him know the family's business. What else, Tom?
en: No, give him a living.  But never let him know the family's business. What else, Tom?

text: I've called the hospital; they've notified Consiglere Genco's family to come and wait.  He won't last out the night.
en: I've called the hospital; they've notified Consiglere Genco's family to come and wait.  He won't last out the night.

text: Tom said he'd take care of it.
en: Tom said he'd take care of it.

text: For bravery.
en: For bravery.

text: And this?
en: And this?

text: For killing a man.
en: For killing a man.

text: What miracles you do for strangers.
en: What miracles you do for strangers.

text: I fought for my country.  It was my choice.
en: I fought for my country.  It was my choice.

text: And now, what do you choose to do?
en: And now, what do you choose to do?

text: I'm going to finish school.
en: I'm going to finish school.

text: Good.  When you are finished, come and talk to me.  I have hopes for you.
en: Good.  When you are finished, come and talk to me.  I have hopes for you.

text: Okay, but just tell him this is one favor I can't give.  But he should try me again on anything else.
en: Okay, but just tell him this is one favor I can't give.  But he should try me again on anything else.

text: He never asks a second favor when he has been refused the first. Understood?
en: He never asks a second favor when he has been refused the first. Understood?

text: You smooth son of a bitch, let me lay it on the line for you, and your boss.  Johnny Fontane never gets that movie.  I don't care how many Dago, Guinea, wop Greaseball Goombahs come out of the woodwork!
en: You smooth son of a bitch, let me lay it on the line for you, and your boss.  Johnny Fontane never gets that movie.  I don't care how many Dago, Guinea, wop Greaseball Goombahs come out of the woodwork!

text: I'm German-Irish.
en: I'm German-Irish.

text: Okay my Kraut-Mick friend, Johnny will never get that part because I hate that pinko punk and I'm going to run him out of the Movies.  And I'll tell you why.  He ruined one of Woltz Brothers' most valuable proteges.  For five years I had this girl under training; singing lessons!  Acting lessons!  Dancing lessons!  We spent hundreds of thousands of dollars--I was going to make her a star.  I'll be even more frank, just to show you that I'm not a hard-hearted man, that it wasn't all dollars and cents.  That girl was beautiful and young and innocent and she was the greatest piece of ass I've ever ad and I've had them all over the world.  Then Johnny comes along with that olive oil voice and guinea charm and she runs off.  She threw it all away to make me look ridiculous.  A MAN IN MY POSITION CANNOT AFFORD TO BE MADE TO LOOK RIDICULOUS!
en: Okay my Kraut-Mick friend, Johnny will never get that part because I hate that pinko punk and I'm going to run him out of the Movies.  And I'll tell you why.  He ruined one of Woltz Brothers' most valuable proteges.  For five years I had this girl under training; singing lessons!  Acting lessons!  Dancing lessons!  We spent hundreds of thousands of dollars--I was going to make her a star.  I'll be even more frank, just to show you that I'm not a hard-hearted man, that it wasn't all dollars and cents.  That girl was beautiful and young and innocent and she was the greatest piece of ass I've ever ad and I've had them all over the world.  Then Johnny comes along with that olive oil voice and guinea charm and she runs off.  She threw it all away to make me look ridiculous.  A MAN IN MY POSITION CANNOT AFFORD TO BE MADE TO LOOK RIDICULOUS!

text: Why do you come to me?  Why do I deserve your generosity?
en: Why do you come to me?  Why do I deserve your generosity?

text: I need two million dollars in cash...more important, I need a friend who has people in high places; a friend who can guarantee that if one of my employees be arrested, they would get only light sentences.  Be my friend.
en: I need two million dollars in cash...more important, I need a friend who has people in high places; a friend who can guarantee that if one of my employees be arrested, they would get only light sentences.  Be my friend.

text: What percentages for my family?
en: What percentages for my family?

text: Thirty per cent.  In the first year your share would be four million dollars; then it would go up.
en: Thirty per cent.  In the first year your share would be four million dollars; then it would go up.

text: And what is the percentage of the Tattaglia family?
en: And what is the percentage of the Tattaglia family?

text: So.  I receive 30 per cent just for finance and legal protection.  No worries about operations, is that what you tell me?
en: So.  I receive 30 per cent just for finance and legal protection.  No worries about operations, is that what you tell me?

text: If you think two million dollars in cash is just finance, I congratulate you Don Corleone.
en: If you think two million dollars in cash is just finance, I congratulate you Don Corleone.

text: Are you worried about security for your million?
en: Are you worried about security for your million?

text: No.
en: No.

text: The Tattaglias will guarantee your investment also.
en: The Tattaglias will guarantee your investment also.

text: It's from Johnny.  It was announced this morning.  He's going to play the lead in the new Woltz Brothers film.
en: It's from Johnny.  It was announced this morning.  He's going to play the lead in the new Woltz Brothers film.

text: And what do you want for Christmas?
en: And what do you want for Christmas?

text: Just you.
en: Just you.

text: As long as I tell him beforehand he won't object.  He'll be hurt, but he won't object.
en: As long as I tell him beforehand he won't object.  He'll be hurt, but he won't object.

text: What time do they expect us?
en: What time do they expect us?

text: For dinner.  Unless I call and tell them we're still in New Hampshire.
en: For dinner.  Unless I call and tell them we're still in New Hampshire.

text: Michael.
en: Michael.

text: Then we can have dinner, see a show, and spend one more night.
en: Then we can have dinner, see a show, and spend one more night.

text: Shhh, you be the long distance operator.  Here.
en: Shhh, you be the long distance operator.  Here.

text: Hello...this is Long Distance.  I have a call from New Hampshire.  Mr. Michael Corleone.  One moment please.
en: Hello...this is Long Distance.  I have a call from New Hampshire.  Mr. Michael Corleone.  One moment please.

text: No.  I guess I'll see you Christmas. Everyone's going to be out at Long Beach, right?
en: No.  I guess I'll see you Christmas. Everyone's going to be out at Long Beach, right?

text: Right.
en: Right.

text: No.
en: No.

text: Would you like me better if I were Ingrid Bergman?
en: Would you like me better if I were Ingrid Bergman?

text: I'm thinking about it.
en: I'm thinking about it.

text: Michael...
en: Michael...

text: No, I would not like you better if you were Ingrid Bergman.
en: No, I would not like you better if you were Ingrid Bergman.

text: Do you recognize my voice?
en: Do you recognize my voice?

text: I think so.  Detective squad?
en: I think so.  Detective squad?

text: Right.  Don't say my name, just listen.  Somebody shot your father outside his place fifteen minutes ago.
en: Right.  Don't say my name, just listen.  Somebody shot your father outside his place fifteen minutes ago.

text: Is he alive?
en: Is he alive?

text: I think so, but I can't get close enough.  There's a lot of blood. I'll try to find out more.
en: I think so, but I can't get close enough.  There's a lot of blood. I'll try to find out more.

text: Find out anything you can...you got a Grand coming.
en: Find out anything you can...you got a Grand coming.

text: Yeah.
en: Yeah.

text: The word is out in the streets that he's dead.
en: The word is out in the streets that he's dead.

text: Where the hell was Paulie, why wasn't he with the Don?
en: Where the hell was Paulie, why wasn't he with the Don?

text: Paulie's been a little sick all winter...he was home.
en: Paulie's been a little sick all winter...he was home.

text: How many times did he stay home the last couple of months?
en: How many times did he stay home the last couple of months?

text: Maybe three, four times.  I always asked Freddie if he wanted another bodyguard, but he said no.  Things have been so smooth the last ten years...
en: Maybe three, four times.  I always asked Freddie if he wanted another bodyguard, but he said no.  Things have been so smooth the last ten years...

text: Go get Paulie, I don't care how sick he is.  Pick him up yourself, and bring him to my father's house.
en: Go get Paulie, I don't care how sick he is.  Pick him up yourself, and bring him to my father's house.

text: That's all?  Don't you want me to send some people over here?
en: That's all?  Don't you want me to send some people over here?

text: No, just you and Paulie.
en: No, just you and Paulie.

text: Sonny will come after you with everything he's got.
en: Sonny will come after you with everything he's got.

text: Even Sonny won't be able to call off Luca Brasi.
en: Even Sonny won't be able to call off Luca Brasi.

text: I'll worry about Luca.  You take care of Sonny and the other two kids.
en: I'll worry about Luca.  You take care of Sonny and the other two kids.

text: I'll try...It's what the Don would want us to do.
en: I'll try...It's what the Don would want us to do.

text: Good...then you can go...  I don't like violence.  I'm a businessman, and blood is a big expense.
en: Good...then you can go...  I don't like violence.  I'm a businessman, and blood is a big expense.

text: How's Mom?
en: How's Mom?

text: Good.  She's been through it before. Me too.  You were too young to know about it.  You better wait outside; there're some things you shouldn't hear.
en: Good.  She's been through it before. Me too.  You were too young to know about it.  You better wait outside; there're some things you shouldn't hear.

text: I can help you out...
en: I can help you out...

text: Oh no you can't, the old man'd be sore as hell if I let you get mixed up in this.
en: Oh no you can't, the old man'd be sore as hell if I let you get mixed up in this.

text: Jesus Christ, he's my father, Sonny.
en: Jesus Christ, he's my father, Sonny.

text: Theresa.
en: Theresa.

text: What?
en: What?

text: One of them fingered the old man.
en: One of them fingered the old man.

text: You're right, kid, Clemenza is okay. It was Paulie.
en: You're right, kid, Clemenza is okay. It was Paulie.

text: How can you be sure?
en: How can you be sure?

text: On the three days Paulie was sick this month, he got calls from a payphone across from the old man's building.  We got people in the phone company.  Thank God it was Paulie...we'll need Clemenza bad.
en: On the three days Paulie was sick this month, he got calls from a payphone across from the old man's building.  We got people in the phone company.  Thank God it was Paulie...we'll need Clemenza bad.

text: Until the old man tells me different.
en: Until the old man tells me different.

text: Then wait, Sonny.  Talk to Pop.
en: Then wait, Sonny.  Talk to Pop.

text: Sollozzo is a dead man, I don't care what it costs.  I don't care if we have to fight all the five families in New York.  The Tattaglia family's going to eat dirt.  I don't care if we all go down together.
en: Sollozzo is a dead man, I don't care what it costs.  I don't care if we have to fight all the five families in New York.  The Tattaglia family's going to eat dirt.  I don't care if we all go down together.

text: That's not how Pop would have played it.
en: That's not how Pop would have played it.

text: I know I'm not the man he was.  But I'll tell you this and he'll tell you too.  When it comes to real action, I can operate as good as anybody short range.
en: I know I'm not the man he was.  But I'll tell you this and he'll tell you too.  When it comes to real action, I can operate as good as anybody short range.

text: All right, Sonny.  All right.
en: All right, Sonny.  All right.

text: Christ, if I could only contact Luca.
en: Christ, if I could only contact Luca.

text: Is it like they say?  Is he that good?
en: Is it like they say?  Is he that good?

text: The cops have it locked in and I got my people there visiting Pop all the time.  What about the hit list.
en: The cops have it locked in and I got my people there visiting Pop all the time.  What about the hit list.

text: If Luca sold out we're in real trouble.
en: If Luca sold out we're in real trouble.

text: Has anyone been able to get in touch with him?
en: Has anyone been able to get in touch with him?

text: No, and I've been calling all night. Maybe he's shacked up.
en: No, and I've been calling all night. Maybe he's shacked up.

text: Luca never sleeps over with a broad. He always goes home when he's through.  Mike, keep ringing Luca's number.
en: Luca never sleeps over with a broad. He always goes home when he's through.  Mike, keep ringing Luca's number.

text: Without your father's political contacts and personal influence, the Corleone family loses half its strength.  Without your father, the other New York families might wind up supporting Sollozzo, and the Tattaglias just to make sure there isn't a long destructive war.  The old days are over, this is 1946; nobody wants bloodshed anymore.  If your father dies...make the deal, Sonny.
en: Without your father's political contacts and personal influence, the Corleone family loses half its strength.  Without your father, the other New York families might wind up supporting Sollozzo, and the Tattaglias just to make sure there isn't a long destructive war.  The old days are over, this is 1946; nobody wants bloodshed anymore.  If your father dies...make the deal, Sonny.

text: That's easy to say; it's not your father.
en: That's easy to say; it's not your father.

text: I was as good a son to him as you or Mike.
en: I was as good a son to him as you or Mike.

text: Oh Christ Tom, I didn't mean it that way.
en: Oh Christ Tom, I didn't mean it that way.

text: We're all tired...
en: We're all tired...

text: OK, we sit tight until the old man can give us the lead.  But Tom, I want you to stay inside the Mall. You too, Mike, no chances.  Tessio, you hold your people in reserve, but have them nosing around the city.  The hospital is yours; I want it tight, fool-proof, 24 hours a day.
en: OK, we sit tight until the old man can give us the lead.  But Tom, I want you to stay inside the Mall. You too, Mike, no chances.  Tessio, you hold your people in reserve, but have them nosing around the city.  The hospital is yours; I want it tight, fool-proof, 24 hours a day.

text: The guy at the gate's outside...says there's a package...
en: The guy at the gate's outside...says there's a package...

text: Sure.
en: Sure.

text: Understood.
en: Understood.

text: Okay, now you can move your men into the Mall, replace Tessio's people.  Mike, tomorrow you take a couple of Clemenza's people and go to Luca's apartment and wait for him to show.  That crazy bastard might be going after Sollozzo right now if he's heard the news.
en: Okay, now you can move your men into the Mall, replace Tessio's people.  Mike, tomorrow you take a couple of Clemenza's people and go to Luca's apartment and wait for him to show.  That crazy bastard might be going after Sollozzo right now if he's heard the news.

text: OK forget it, just stay on the phone.
en: OK forget it, just stay on the phone.

text: Drive while you thinking; I wanna get to the City this month!
en: Drive while you thinking; I wanna get to the City this month!

text: OK, go to Arthur Avenue; I'm suppose to call when I found somethin'.
en: OK, go to Arthur Avenue; I'm suppose to call when I found somethin'.

text: Maybe, or you gotta know now.
en: Maybe, or you gotta know now.

text: Holy cow, I don't gotta know nothing.
en: Holy cow, I don't gotta know nothing.

text: How is your father?
en: How is your father?

text: He'll be OK.
en: He'll be OK.

text: I love you.
en: I love you.

text: Yeah Kay, I'm here.
en: Yeah Kay, I'm here.

text: Can you say it?
en: Can you say it?

text: Huh?
en: Huh?

text: Tell me you love me.
en: Tell me you love me.

text: Please say it.
en: Please say it.

text: Look.  I'll see you tonight, OK?
en: Look.  I'll see you tonight, OK?

text: OK.
en: OK.

text: You won't see Paulie anymore.  He's sick for good this winter.
en: You won't see Paulie anymore.  He's sick for good this winter.

text: To the city.
en: To the city.

text: Send some bodyguards.
en: Send some bodyguards.

text: I don't need them, Sonny.  I'm just going to see Pop in the hospital. Also, I got other things.
en: I don't need them, Sonny.  I'm just going to see Pop in the hospital. Also, I got other things.

text: OK, but be careful.
en: OK, but be careful.

text: Can I go to the hospital with you?
en: Can I go to the hospital with you?

text: I don't think so.  You don't want to end up on page 3 of the Daily News.
en: I don't think so.  You don't want to end up on page 3 of the Daily News.

text: My parents don't read the Daily News.  All right, if you think I shouldn't.  I can't believe the things the papers are printing. I'm sure most of it's not true.
en: My parents don't read the Daily News.  All right, if you think I shouldn't.  I can't believe the things the papers are printing. I'm sure most of it's not true.

text: I don't think so either.  I better go.
en: I don't think so either.  I better go.

text: When will I see you again?
en: When will I see you again?

text: I want you to go back to New Hampshire...think things over.
en: I want you to go back to New Hampshire...think things over.

text: Goodbye.
en: Goodbye.

text: Oh your father just had too many visitors.  It interfered with the hospital service.  The police came and made them all leave just ten minutes ago.  But don't worry.  I look in on him.
en: Oh your father just had too many visitors.  It interfered with the hospital service.  The police came and made them all leave just ten minutes ago.  But don't worry.  I look in on him.

text: You just stand here one minute...
en: You just stand here one minute...

text: All right, get him in a different room; lock the door from the inside. I'll have some men there inside of fifteen minutes.  Sit tight, and don't panic.
en: All right, get him in a different room; lock the door from the inside. I'll have some men there inside of fifteen minutes.  Sit tight, and don't panic.

text: I won't panic.
en: I won't panic.

text: You and I are going to move my father right now...to another room on another floor...Can you disconnect those tubes so we can wheel the bed out?
en: You and I are going to move my father right now...to another room on another floor...Can you disconnect those tubes so we can wheel the bed out?

text: Absolutely not!  We have to get permission from the Doctor.
en: Absolutely not!  We have to get permission from the Doctor.

text: You've read about my father in the papers.  You've seen that no one's here to guard him.  Now I've just gotten word that men are coming to this hospital to kill him.  Believe me and help me.
en: You've read about my father in the papers.  You've seen that no one's here to guard him.  Now I've just gotten word that men are coming to this hospital to kill him.  Believe me and help me.

text: We don't have to disconnect them, we can wheel the stand with the bed.
en: We don't have to disconnect them, we can wheel the stand with the bed.

text: Cut it out.
en: Cut it out.

text: The Turk wants to talk!  The nerve of that son of a bitch!  After he craps out last night he wants a meet.
en: The Turk wants to talk!  The nerve of that son of a bitch!  After he craps out last night he wants a meet.

text: Sure, he wants us to send Mike to meet him to hear his proposition. The promise is the deal will be so good we can't refuse.
en: Sure, he wants us to send Mike to meet him to hear his proposition. The promise is the deal will be so good we can't refuse.

text: What about that Tattaglias?  What will they do about Bruno?
en: What about that Tattaglias?  What will they do about Bruno?

text: Part of the deal: Bruno cancels out what they did to my father.
en: Part of the deal: Bruno cancels out what they did to my father.

text: We should hear what they have to say.
en: We should hear what they have to say.

text: No, no Consiglere.  Not this time. No more meetings, no more discussions, no more Sollozzo tricks.  Give them one message: I WANT SOLLOZZO.  If not, it's all out war.  We go to the mattresses and we put all the button men out on the street.
en: No, no Consiglere.  Not this time. No more meetings, no more discussions, no more Sollozzo tricks.  Give them one message: I WANT SOLLOZZO.  If not, it's all out war.  We go to the mattresses and we put all the button men out on the street.

text: The other families won't sit still for all out war.
en: The other families won't sit still for all out war.

text: Then THEY hand me Sollozzo.
en: Then THEY hand me Sollozzo.

text: Come ON Sonny, your father wouldn't want to hear this.  This is not a personal thing, this is Business.
en: Come ON Sonny, your father wouldn't want to hear this.  This is not a personal thing, this is Business.

text: And when they shot me father...
en: And when they shot me father...

text: Yes, even the shooting of your father was business, not personal...
en: Yes, even the shooting of your father was business, not personal...

text: No no, no more advice on how to patch it up Tom.  You just help me win.  Understood?
en: No no, no more advice on how to patch it up Tom.  You just help me win.  Understood?

text: McCluskey can't stay with the Turk forever.  We'll wait.
en: McCluskey can't stay with the Turk forever.  We'll wait.

text: The kid's right.
en: The kid's right.

text: Let's say now that we have to kill McCluskey.  We'll clear that up through our Newspaper contacts later.
en: Let's say now that we have to kill McCluskey.  We'll clear that up through our Newspaper contacts later.

text: They want me to go to the conference with Sollozzo.  Set up the meeting for two days from now.  Sonny, get our informers to find out where the meeting will be held. Insist it has to be a public place: a bar or restaurant at the height of the dinner hour.  So I'll feel safe.  They'll check me when I meet them so I won't be able to carry a weapon; but Clemenza, figure out a way to have one planted there for me.  Then I'll kill them both.
en: They want me to go to the conference with Sollozzo.  Set up the meeting for two days from now.  Sonny, get our informers to find out where the meeting will be held. Insist it has to be a public place: a bar or restaurant at the height of the dinner hour.  So I'll feel safe.  They'll check me when I meet them so I won't be able to carry a weapon; but Clemenza, figure out a way to have one planted there for me.  Then I'll kill them both.

text: How bad will it be?
en: How bad will it be?

text: Probably all the other families will line up against us.  But, it's alright.  These things have to happen once every ten years or so...gets rid of the bad blood. You gotta stop 'em at the beginning. Like they shoulda stopped Hitler at Munich, they shoulda never let him get away with that, they were just asking for big trouble...
en: Probably all the other families will line up against us.  But, it's alright.  These things have to happen once every ten years or so...gets rid of the bad blood. You gotta stop 'em at the beginning. Like they shoulda stopped Hitler at Munich, they shoulda never let him get away with that, they were just asking for big trouble...

text: What are you, inspecting the barracks?  You ready?  Did Clemenza tell you be sure to drop the gun right away?
en: What are you, inspecting the barracks?  You ready?  Did Clemenza tell you be sure to drop the gun right away?

text: A million times.
en: A million times.

text: Sollozzo and McCluskey are going to pick you up in an hour and a half on Times Square, under the big Camels sign.
en: Sollozzo and McCluskey are going to pick you up in an hour and a half on Times Square, under the big Camels sign.

text: No.  Our informer in McCluskey's precinct.  Tonight at 8:00 he signed out for Louis' Restaurant in the Bronx.  Anyone know it.
en: No.  Our informer in McCluskey's precinct.  Tonight at 8:00 he signed out for Louis' Restaurant in the Bronx.  Anyone know it.

text: The gun will be there.
en: The gun will be there.

text: You're on, kid...I'll square it with Mom your not seeing her before you left.  And I'll get a message to your girl friend when I think the time is right.
en: You're on, kid...I'll square it with Mom your not seeing her before you left.  And I'll get a message to your girl friend when I think the time is right.

text: We gotta move...
en: We gotta move...

text: Probably a year...
en: Probably a year...

text: Can you do it Mike?
en: Can you do it Mike?

text: I want to settle things tonight.  I want my father left alone.
en: I want to settle things tonight.  I want my father left alone.

text: He won't be; I swear to you be my children he won't be.  Just keep an open mind when we talk.  I hope you're not a hothead like your brother, Sonny.  It's impossible to talk business with him.
en: He won't be; I swear to you be my children he won't be.  Just keep an open mind when we talk.  I hope you're not a hothead like your brother, Sonny.  It's impossible to talk business with him.

text: Maybe.
en: Maybe.

text: What guarantees can I give you?  I am the hunted one.  I've missed my chance.  You think too highly of me, my friend...I am not so clever...all I want if a truce...
en: What guarantees can I give you?  I am the hunted one.  I've missed my chance.  You think too highly of me, my friend...I am not so clever...all I want if a truce...

text: Is it all right if I go to the bathroom?
en: Is it all right if I go to the bathroom?

text: I've tried calling and writing.  I want to reach Michael.
en: I've tried calling and writing.  I want to reach Michael.

text: Nobody knows where he is.  We know he's all right, but that's all.
en: Nobody knows where he is.  We know he's all right, but that's all.

text: An accident.  No one was hurt.
en: An accident.  No one was hurt.

text: Listen Tom, I let my cab go; can I come in to call another one?
en: Listen Tom, I let my cab go; can I come in to call another one?

text: If I accept that letter and you told a Court of Law I accepted it, they would interpret it as my having knowledge of his whereabouts. Just wait Kay, he'll contact you.
en: If I accept that letter and you told a Court of Law I accepted it, they would interpret it as my having knowledge of his whereabouts. Just wait Kay, he'll contact you.

text: Mama, no.
en: Mama, no.

text: Shut up.
en: Shut up.

text: We put out a lot of material through our contacts in the Newspapers...about McCluskey's being tied up with Sollozzo in the Drug Rackets...things are starting to loosen up.
en: We put out a lot of material through our contacts in the Newspapers...about McCluskey's being tied up with Sollozzo in the Drug Rackets...things are starting to loosen up.

text: You start operating, the five families will start their raids again.  We're at a stalemate Sonny, your war is costing us a lot of money.
en: You start operating, the five families will start their raids again.  We're at a stalemate Sonny, your war is costing us a lot of money.

text: No more stalemate Tom, we got the soldiers, we'll match them gun for gun if that's how they want it. They know me for what I am, Tom-- and they're scared of me.
en: No more stalemate Tom, we got the soldiers, we'll match them gun for gun if that's how they want it. They know me for what I am, Tom-- and they're scared of me.

text: Yes.  That's true, you're getting a hell of a reputation.
en: Yes.  That's true, you're getting a hell of a reputation.

text: Well it's war!  We might not be in this shape if we had a real war- time Consiglere, a Sicilian.  Pop had Genco, who do I have?  Hey Tom, hey...hey.  It's Sunday, we're gonna have dinner.  Don't be sore.
en: Well it's war!  We might not be in this shape if we had a real war- time Consiglere, a Sicilian.  Pop had Genco, who do I have?  Hey Tom, hey...hey.  It's Sunday, we're gonna have dinner.  Don't be sore.

text: Your girl friend.  She says she can't make it tonight.  You lousy bastard you have the nerve to give your whores my telephone number. I'll kill you, you bastard!
en: Your girl friend.  She says she can't make it tonight.  You lousy bastard you have the nerve to give your whores my telephone number. I'll kill you, you bastard!

text: OK, OK.  You gonna make me something to eat at least?
en: OK, OK.  You gonna make me something to eat at least?

text: I'm not hungry yet.
en: I'm not hungry yet.

text: Eat it, it's on the table.
en: Eat it, it's on the table.

text: Ba Fa Goulle.
en: Ba Fa Goulle.

text: BA FA GOULE YOU!
en: BA FA GOULE YOU!

text: Like hell I will.
en: Like hell I will.

text: You owe the Don a service.  In one hour, not before, perhaps later, he will be at your funeral parlor to ask for your help.  Be there to greet him.  If you have any objections speak now, and I'll inform him.
en: You owe the Don a service.  In one hour, not before, perhaps later, he will be at your funeral parlor to ask for your help.  Be there to greet him.  If you have any objections speak now, and I'll inform him.

text: Good.  He never doubted you.
en: Good.  He never doubted you.

text: The Don himself is coming to me tonight?
en: The Don himself is coming to me tonight?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: I want you to use all your powers, all your skill, as you love me.  I do not want his mother to see him as he is.
en: I want you to use all your powers, all your skill, as you love me.  I do not want his mother to see him as he is.

text: I didn't tell Mama anything.  I was about to come up and wake you and tell you.  Just now.
en: I didn't tell Mama anything.  I was about to come up and wake you and tell you.  Just now.

text: But you needed a drink first.
en: But you needed a drink first.

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Now you've had your drink.
en: Now you've had your drink.

text: Yes, I understand I'm listening.
en: Yes, I understand I'm listening.

text: You owe the Don a service.  He has no doubt that you will repay it.
en: You owe the Don a service.  He has no doubt that you will repay it.

text: Mention it, don't insist.  Barzini is a man who will know that without being told.
en: Mention it, don't insist.  Barzini is a man who will know that without being told.

text: You mean Tattaglia.
en: You mean Tattaglia.

text: Barzini.
en: Barzini.

text: He was the one behind Sollozzo?
en: He was the one behind Sollozzo?

text: Tattaglia is a pimp.  He could never have outfought Santino.  But I wasn't sure until this day.  No, it was Barzini all along.
en: Tattaglia is a pimp.  He could never have outfought Santino.  But I wasn't sure until this day.  No, it was Barzini all along.

text: Michael, why must you do this.  We have been lucky so far, all these months you've been here we've kept your name a secret.  It is from love for your father that I've asked you never to more than an hour from the Villa.
en: Michael, why must you do this.  We have been lucky so far, all these months you've been here we've kept your name a secret.  It is from love for your father that I've asked you never to more than an hour from the Villa.

text: Calo and Fabrizzio are with me; nothing will happen.
en: Calo and Fabrizzio are with me; nothing will happen.

text: You must understand that your Father's enemies have friends in Palermo.
en: You must understand that your Father's enemies have friends in Palermo.

text: I know.
en: I know.

text: Where are you going?
en: Where are you going?

text: Corleone.
en: Corleone.

text: There is nothing there.  Not anymore.
en: There is nothing there.  Not anymore.

text: I was told that my Grandfather was murdered on its main street; and his murderers came to kill my father there when he was twelve years old.
en: I was told that my Grandfather was murdered on its main street; and his murderers came to kill my father there when he was twelve years old.

text: Long ago.  Now there is nothing: the men killed each other in family vendettas...the others escaped to America.
en: Long ago.  Now there is nothing: the men killed each other in family vendettas...the others escaped to America.

text: Don Tommassino...I should see this place.
en: Don Tommassino...I should see this place.

text: No...I would like to walk to Corleone.
en: No...I would like to walk to Corleone.

text: How do you know I come from America?
en: How do you know I come from America?

text: We hear.  We were told you were a Pezzonovanta...big shot.
en: We hear.  We were told you were a Pezzonovanta...big shot.

text: Only the son of a Pezzonovanta.
en: Only the son of a Pezzonovanta.

text: Hey America!  Is she as rich as they say?
en: Hey America!  Is she as rich as they say?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Take me to America!  You need a good lupara in America?  You take me, I'll be the best man you got.  "Oh say, can you seeee...By da star early light..."
en: Take me to America!  You need a good lupara in America?  You take me, I'll be the best man you got.  "Oh say, can you seeee...By da star early light..."

text: Shhhhh.
en: Shhhhh.

text: This one could seduce the devil.  A body! and eyes as big and black as olives.
en: This one could seduce the devil.  A body! and eyes as big and black as olives.

text: I know about what you mean!
en: I know about what you mean!

text: This was a beauty.  Right, Calo?
en: This was a beauty.  Right, Calo?

text: Beautiful all over, eh?
en: Beautiful all over, eh?

text: And hair.  Black and curly, like a doll.  And such a mouth.
en: And hair.  Black and curly, like a doll.  And such a mouth.

text: Come Sunday morning:  My name is Vitelli and my house is up there on the hill, above the village.
en: Come Sunday morning:  My name is Vitelli and my house is up there on the hill, above the village.

text: The younger men have no respect. Things are changing; I don't know what will happen.  Michael, because of the wedding, people now know your name.
en: The younger men have no respect. Things are changing; I don't know what will happen.  Michael, because of the wedding, people now know your name.

text: Is that why there are more men on the walls?
en: Is that why there are more men on the walls?

text: Even so, I don't think it is safe here anymore.  I've made plans to move you to a villa near Siracuse. You must go right away.
en: Even so, I don't think it is safe here anymore.  I've made plans to move you to a villa near Siracuse. You must go right away.

text: What is it?
en: What is it?

text: Bad news from America.  Your brother, Santino.  He has been killed.
en: Bad news from America.  Your brother, Santino.  He has been killed.

text: Calo is having a cup of coffee in the kitchen.  Is your wife coming with you?
en: Calo is having a cup of coffee in the kitchen.  Is your wife coming with you?

text: No, she's going home to her family. She'll join me in a few weeks...
en: No, she's going home to her family. She'll join me in a few weeks...

text: No.
en: No.

text: I understand, Michael.  But you must make a family, you know.
en: I understand, Michael.  But you must make a family, you know.

text: I want children, I want a family. But I don't know when.
en: I want children, I want a family. But I don't know when.

text: Accept what's happened, Michael.
en: Accept what's happened, Michael.

text: I could accept everything that's happened; I could accept it, but that I never had a choice.  From the time I was born, you had laid this all out for me.
en: I could accept everything that's happened; I could accept it, but that I never had a choice.  From the time I was born, you had laid this all out for me.

text: No, I wanted other things for you.
en: No, I wanted other things for you.

text: You wanted me to be your son.
en: You wanted me to be your son.

text: Yes, but sons who would be professors, scientists, musicians...and grandchildren who could be, who knows, a Governor, a President even, nothing's impossible here in America.
en: Yes, but sons who would be professors, scientists, musicians...and grandchildren who could be, who knows, a Governor, a President even, nothing's impossible here in America.

text: Then why have I become a man like you?
en: Then why have I become a man like you?

text: You are like me, we refuse to be fools, to be puppets dancing on a string pulled by other men.  I hoped the time for guns and killing and massacres was over.  That was my misfortune.  That was your misfortune.  I was hunted on the streets of Corleone when I was twelve years old because of who my father was.  I had no choice.
en: You are like me, we refuse to be fools, to be puppets dancing on a string pulled by other men.  I hoped the time for guns and killing and massacres was over.  That was my misfortune.  That was your misfortune.  I was hunted on the streets of Corleone when I was twelve years old because of who my father was.  I had no choice.

text: A man has to choose what he will be. I believe that.
en: A man has to choose what he will be. I believe that.

text: What else do you believe in?
en: What else do you believe in?

text: He had business at the hotel, but he'll drop in for dinner.
en: He had business at the hotel, but he'll drop in for dinner.

text: You look good, too.
en: You look good, too.

text: No.
en: No.

text: Come here a minute, Fredo.
en: Come here a minute, Fredo.

text: That's for you to find out.
en: That's for you to find out.

text: Give them some money and send them home.
en: Give them some money and send them home.

text: Mike!
en: Mike!

text: Get rid of them...
en: Get rid of them...

text: I'll make him an offer he can't refuse.
en: I'll make him an offer he can't refuse.

text: Thanks.
en: Thanks.

text: The chef cooked for you special; the dancers will kick your tongue out and you credit is good!  Draw chips for all these people so they can play on the house.
en: The chef cooked for you special; the dancers will kick your tongue out and you credit is good!  Draw chips for all these people so they can play on the house.

text: Is my credit good enough to buy you out?
en: Is my credit good enough to buy you out?

text: The hotel, the casino.  The Corleone family wants to buy you out.
en: The hotel, the casino.  The Corleone family wants to buy you out.

text: Your casino loses money.  Maybe we can do better.
en: Your casino loses money.  Maybe we can do better.

text: You think I scam?
en: You think I scam?

text: You're unlucky.
en: You're unlucky.

text: You goddamn dagos.  I do you a favor and take Freddie in when you're having a bad time, and then you try to push me out.
en: You goddamn dagos.  I do you a favor and take Freddie in when you're having a bad time, and then you try to push me out.

text: You took Freddie in because the Corleone family bankrolled your casino.  You and the Corleone family are evened out.  This is for business; name your price.
en: You took Freddie in because the Corleone family bankrolled your casino.  You and the Corleone family are evened out.  This is for business; name your price.

text: The Corleone family don't have that kind of muscle anymore.  The Godfather is sick.  You're getting chased out of New York by Barzini and the other families, and you think you can find easier pickings here.  I've talked to Barzini; I can make a deal with him and keep my hotel!
en: The Corleone family don't have that kind of muscle anymore.  The Godfather is sick.  You're getting chased out of New York by Barzini and the other families, and you think you can find easier pickings here.  I've talked to Barzini; I can make a deal with him and keep my hotel!

text: Is that why you thought you could slap Freddie around in public?
en: Is that why you thought you could slap Freddie around in public?

text: Hell, he was banging cocktail waitresses two at a time.  Players couldn't get a drink.
en: Hell, he was banging cocktail waitresses two at a time.  Players couldn't get a drink.

text: You son of a bitch, you think you can brush me off like that?  I made my bones when you were going out with cheerleaders.
en: You son of a bitch, you think you can brush me off like that?  I made my bones when you were going out with cheerleaders.

text: The Don has semi-retired.  I'm running the Family business now. So anything you have to say, say it to me.
en: The Don has semi-retired.  I'm running the Family business now. So anything you have to say, say it to me.

text: Oh Michael.
en: Oh Michael.

text: We'll go to the show tomorrow night--we can change the tickets.
en: We'll go to the show tomorrow night--we can change the tickets.

text: Don't you want dinner first?
en: Don't you want dinner first?

text: No, you eat...don't wait up for me.
en: No, you eat...don't wait up for me.

text: Wake me up when you come to bed?
en: Wake me up when you come to bed?

text: Let HER ask.
en: Let HER ask.

text: I'm busy.
en: I'm busy.

text: Connie and Carlo want you to be godfather to their little boy.
en: Connie and Carlo want you to be godfather to their little boy.

text: Let me think about it, O.K.?
en: Let me think about it, O.K.?

text: Just be patient.
en: Just be patient.

text: I'm not asking you for help, Mike. Just take off the handcuffs.
en: I'm not asking you for help, Mike. Just take off the handcuffs.

text: Be patient.
en: Be patient.

text: No, I don't want to give Barzini an excuse to start fighting.
en: No, I don't want to give Barzini an excuse to start fighting.

text: Then in a six month time we're on our own; is that it?
en: Then in a six month time we're on our own; is that it?

text: Maybe less...
en: Maybe less...

text: No.  I want things very calm for another six months.
en: No.  I want things very calm for another six months.

text: Forgive me, Godfather, let our years of friendship be my excuse. How can you hope for success there without your strength here to back you up?  The two  go hand in hand. And with you gone from here the Barzini and the Tattaglias will be too strong for us.
en: Forgive me, Godfather, let our years of friendship be my excuse. How can you hope for success there without your strength here to back you up?  The two  go hand in hand. And with you gone from here the Barzini and the Tattaglias will be too strong for us.

text: I'll come to you when I need you.
en: I'll come to you when I need you.

text: Tom, we're going to be legitimate all the way, and you're the legal man.  What could be more important than that.
en: Tom, we're going to be legitimate all the way, and you're the legal man.  What could be more important than that.

text: I'm not talking about that.  I'm talking about Rocco Lampone building a secret regime.  Why does Neri report directly to you, rather than through me or a caporegime?
en: I'm not talking about that.  I'm talking about Rocco Lampone building a secret regime.  Why does Neri report directly to you, rather than through me or a caporegime?

text: How did you find out?
en: How did you find out?

text: Not so perfectly if you noticed.
en: Not so perfectly if you noticed.

text: Mike, why am I out?
en: Mike, why am I out?

text: You're not a wartime Consigliere. Things may get tough with the move we're trying.
en: You're not a wartime Consigliere. Things may get tough with the move we're trying.

text: OK, but then I agree with Tessio. You're going about it all wrong; you're making the move out of weakness... Barzini's a wolf, and if he tears you apart, the other families won't come running to help the Corleones...
en: OK, but then I agree with Tessio. You're going about it all wrong; you're making the move out of weakness... Barzini's a wolf, and if he tears you apart, the other families won't come running to help the Corleones...

text: Maybe I can help.
en: Maybe I can help.

text: I'll need him.
en: I'll need him.

text: There are men in this world who demand to be killed.  They argue in gambling games; they jump out of their cars in a rage if someone so much as scratches their fender. These people wander through the streets calling out "Kill me, kill me."  Luca Brasi was like that. And since he wasn't scared of death, and in fact, looked for it...I made him my weapon.  Because I was the only person in the world that he truly hoped would not kill him.  I think you have done the same with this man.
en: There are men in this world who demand to be killed.  They argue in gambling games; they jump out of their cars in a rage if someone so much as scratches their fender. These people wander through the streets calling out "Kill me, kill me."  Luca Brasi was like that. And since he wasn't scared of death, and in fact, looked for it...I made him my weapon.  Because I was the only person in the world that he truly hoped would not kill him.  I think you have done the same with this man.

text: How?
en: How?

text: He will get in touch with you through someone you absolutely trust.  That person will arrange a meeting, guarantee your safety...
en: He will get in touch with you through someone you absolutely trust.  That person will arrange a meeting, guarantee your safety...

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Good.
en: Good.

text: He talked to you?
en: He talked to you?

text: I can arrange security.
en: I can arrange security.

text: Did he give you his politicians?
en: Did he give you his politicians?

text: Not all...I needed another four months and I would have had them all.  I guess you've figured it all out?
en: Not all...I needed another four months and I would have had them all.  I guess you've figured it all out?

text: How will they come at you?
en: How will they come at you?

text: I know now.  I'll make them call me Don.
en: I know now.  I'll make them call me Don.

text: Have you agreed on a meeting?
en: Have you agreed on a meeting?

text: A week from tonight.  In Brooklyn on Tessio's ground, where I'll be safe.
en: A week from tonight.  In Brooklyn on Tessio's ground, where I'll be safe.

text: Yes, I promise.
en: Yes, I promise.

text: I do renounce him.
en: I do renounce him.

text: And all his works?
en: And all his works?

text: I do renounce them.
en: I do renounce them.

text: I do wish to be baptized.
en: I do wish to be baptized.

text: Go back to your house and wait for me...
en: Go back to your house and wait for me...

text: You have to answer for Santino.
en: You have to answer for Santino.

text: I swear I'm innocent.  I swear on the head of my children, I'm innocent.  Mike, don't do this to me, please Mike, don't do this to me!
en: I swear I'm innocent.  I swear on the head of my children, I'm innocent.  Mike, don't do this to me, please Mike, don't do this to me!

text: Barzini is dead.  So is Philip Tattaglia, so are Strachi, Cuneo and Moe Greene...I want to square all the family accounts tonight. So don't tell me you're innocent; admit what you did.
en: Barzini is dead.  So is Philip Tattaglia, so are Strachi, Cuneo and Moe Greene...I want to square all the family accounts tonight. So don't tell me you're innocent; admit what you did.

text: Barzini.
en: Barzini.

text: Good, good.  Leave now; there's a car waiting to take you to the airport.
en: Good, good.  Leave now; there's a car waiting to take you to the airport.

text: Don't ask me.
en: Don't ask me.

text: Tell me!
en: Tell me!

text: All right, this one time I'll let you ask about my affairs, one last time.
en: All right, this one time I'll let you ask about my affairs, one last time.

text: Is it true?
en: Is it true?
